zotero.abstractNote: Musculoskeletal robots are characterized by their high-dimensional, redundant, and non-linear dynamics, which makes them difficult to control. Motor babbling is a common approach to learn the forward dynamics of such systems. However, global exploration is often inefficient due to the high dimensionality of the motor space. In this paper, we propose a local online motor babbling approach that exploits the motor abundance of a musculoskeletal robot arm. We show that by focusing exploration on the null space of the task, we can learn the forward dynamics more efficiently. We evaluate our approach on a musculoskeletal robot arm and show that it outperforms global motor babbling in terms of learning speed and accuracy.
zotero.conferenceName: International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: 2019 IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
